ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chiffon cake entry (FOODON_03301116); no pandan-specific chiffon cake entry exists. Closest parent used.

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for pandan chiffon cake or chiffon cake as a food product. None assigned.

preparation_method: whisked egg whites folded into batter

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard slice of pandan chiffon cake weighs approximately 60 g, representing roughly one-tenth of a standard 600 g chiffon cake ring.

nutrient_source.title: Pandan Chiffon Cake — HPB Singapore Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo database lists pandan chiffon cake as a distinct entry. Per-100 g values sourced directly from that entry. Serving size of 60 g is consistent with HPB standard slice reference.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Chiffon Cake and Analogous Sponge-Type Cakes — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for pandan chiffon cake exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. The GI of 67 is estimated from the closest analogous entry: sponge cake / chiffon-type cake (GI ~67, medium category) as listed in the Sydney GI Database. Pandan chiffon cake shares a similar flour-egg-sugar-oil matrix with minimal fibre, supporting this estimate. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (67 × 32.1) / 100 ≈ 21.5, using 60 g serving with 53.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (210 mg per 100 g) and moderate energy density
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit pandan chiffon cake to occasional small portions, as a 60 g slice contributes approximately 126 mg sodium; cumulative intake across a meal can approach clinically relevant thresholds when combined with other sodium-containing foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content with medium-high glycemic index (GI 67) and glycemic load of 21.5 per slice
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat pandan chiffon cake as an occasional indulgence only; a single 60 g slice delivers approximately 32 g of rapidly absorbed carbohydrate with a glycemic load of 21.5, which can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation and should be accounted for within the daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (3.8 g per 100 g), cholesterol (95 mg per 100 g), and added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, pandan chiffon cake should be consumed sparingly; a 60 g slice provides approximately 2.3 g saturated fat and 57 mg cholesterol, and the high sugar content contributes to triglyceride elevation risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pandan chiffon cake is a light, airy Southeast Asian baked dessert that is especially popular in Singapore, Malaysia, and Indonesia. It is made by folding stiffly beaten egg whites into a batter of wheat flour, egg yolks, sugar, vegetable oil, coconut milk, and freshly extracted pandan (screwpine leaf) juice, which gives the cake its characteristic bright green colour and fragrant, subtly sweet, grassy aroma. The cake is baked in a tube pan and inverted while cooling to preserve its tall, cloud-like structure. The texture is exceptionally soft, moist, and springy — lighter than a conventional butter cake but richer than a plain angel food cake. Pandan chiffon cake is a beloved everyday dessert and teatime snack in Singapore, commonly sold at kopitiams, bakeries, and hawker centres, and is considered a national comfort food. Nutritionally, a 60 g slice provides approximately 203 kcal, 32 g carbohydrate, 6.7 g fat, and 3.7 g protein. The glycemic index is estimated at 67 (medium), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 21.5 per slice, making it a food that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume only occasionally and in controlled portions. The sodium content of 210 mg per 100 g is moderate and relevant for hypertension management. The cake is dairy-free and halal-suitable, making it accessible to a wide range of dietary preferences in Singapore's multicultural food landscape.

confidence_reason: HPB Singapore Foodo is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes and lists pandan chiffon cake as a distinct entry. Per-100 g macronutrient values are sourced from that entry. Some micronutrient fields (vitamin B6, B12, folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, selenium) are not reported in the HPB entry and are set to -1. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (sponge/chiffon cake category) rather than a direct measurement for pandan chiffon cake, which reduces confidence from high to medium.
notes: Vitamin B6 is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for pandan chiffon cake. Vitamin B12 is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry. GI value of 67 is an analogous estimate based on sponge/chiffon cake entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for pandan chiffon cake was found. Serving size of 60 g is consistent with HPB standard slice reference and Toast Box portion context. The cuisine field is set to 'Singaporean' rather than 'Toast' (the field survey cuisine tag) as pandan chiffon cake is a Singaporean dish; 'Toast' refers to the restaurant category on the delivery platform. All aliases_by_language entries are confirmed present verbatim in the flat aliases list.
